subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the hybrid nature of carbon bonds and the geometry of allotropes
Master IUPAC nomenclature for polyfunctional and branched compounds
Explain the thermodynamics and mechanisms of Esterification and Saponification
Differentiate between structural, chain, and position isomerism with complex examples
Evaluate the oxidative and reductive pathways of alcohols and carboxylic acids
Analyze the chemical kinetics of soap action and the coordination chemistry of scum formation

lesson_plan.title: Covalent Bonding & Geometric Isomerism
topics_covered: Nature of covalent bonds: Single, Double, and Triple
Tetravalency and the 109.5° bond angle (Basic intro to 3D geometry)
Structural vs. Position Isomerism in Butene and Pentene
teaching_strategy: 3D Molecular Model demonstration
Analyzing why Carbon doesn't form $C^{4+}$ or $C^{4-}$ ions
Visualizing the 'pi' and 'sigma' bond concept (simplified)
student_activities: Draw all 5 structural isomers of Hexane ($C_{6}H_{14}$)
Calculate the total number of shared electron pairs in Benzene
assessment: Oral quiz on bond angles and stability

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Nomenclature & Polyfunctional Groups
topics_covered: IUPAC rules for compounds with multiple functional groups
Priority order of functional groups (Carboxylic acid > Alcohol > Halogen)
teaching_strategy: The 'Main Chain' selection algorithm
Drills on numbering from the end nearest to the priority group
Decoding complex names into structures

lesson_plan.title: Oxidation and Addition Mechanisms
teaching_strategy: Step-by-step electron transfer in oxidation
Comparison: Why saturated fats are solid and unsaturated are liquid
Discussion on why addition is an 'atom economy' favorable reaction
student_activities: Identify the oxidizing agent in the conversion of Propanol to Propanoic acid
Predict the product of 'HBr' addition to Propene (Intro to Markovnikov logic)
assessment: Short answer: Role of catalyst in hydrogenation

lesson_plan.title: Chemistry of Ethanol: Reactions & Safety
topics_covered: Dehydration of ethanol to ethene ($H_{2}SO_{4}$ at 443K)
Reaction with Sodium: Formation of Sodium Ethoxide
teaching_strategy: Temperature-dependent reactions of ethanol
The 'Pop' test for Hydrogen during Sodium reaction
Real-world case study: The chemistry of hangover and liver cirrhosis
student_activities: Write the mechanism for ethanol dehydration
Research: Why is Methanol considered a poison at the molecular level?

lesson_plan.title: Ethanoic Acid and Esterification Dynamics
topics_covered: Esterification: Reaction of acid and alcohol
teaching_strategy: The 'Sweet-smelling' ester experiment in the lab
Analyzing why the reaction is reversible
Mechanism of the reaction with Carbonates ($NaHCO_{3}$/ $Na_{2}CO_{3}$)
student_activities: Perform the Esterification reaction and smell the product
Observe the effervescence of $CO_{2}$ and record the balanced equation
assessment: HOTS: Identifying 'Compound A' and 'Compound B' in an ester puzzle

lesson_plan.title: Saponification and Micelle Kinetics
topics_covered: Thermodynamics of Hydrophilic vs. Hydrophobic ends
teaching_strategy: Soap making demonstration using vegetable oil and $NaOH$
Analyzing the 'Tyndall effect' in soap solutions
Molecular animation of grease trapping
student_activities: Prepare a small sample of soap in a test tube
Draw a cross-section of a micelle trapping a dirt particle
assessment: Explaining the role of the ionic 'head' in water solubility

lesson_plan.title: Hard Water and Detergent Analysis
topics_covered: Scum formation: Reaction of soap with $Ca^{2+}$ and $Mg^{2+}$ ions
teaching_strategy: Experimental comparison: Lather formation in hard vs. distilled water
Analyzing the chemical formula of Scum (Calcium Stearate)
Comprehensive revision through Assertion-Reasoning questions
student_activities: Test the 'cleansing capacity' of soap vs. detergent in hard water
assessment: Review of logic in multi-step chemical conversions

teaching_aids: Advanced Molecular Model Kit (incl. lone pairs)
Chemicals: Glacial Acetic Acid, Ethanol, Conc. $H_{2}SO_{4}$, $NaOH$, $KMnO_{4}$
NCERT Exemplar and IIT-JEE Foundation material
teacher_tip: At the 'Hard' level, students often struggle with the reversibility of Esterification. Teach them the 'Le Chatelier' concept (simply) by explaining that removing water as it forms helps drive the reaction toward the ester.
